prompt: Every task in the current build wave is already marked done, and the deduped review scope now contains 8 files across two logical areas: `src/auth/login.ts`, `src/auth/session.ts`, `src/auth/token.ts`, `tests/auth/login.test.ts`, `src/billing/invoice.ts`, `src/billing/payment.ts`, `src/billing/refund.ts`, and `tests/billing/invoice.test.ts`. Using the build workflow, how should the manager launch code-reviewers before the final tracking sync?
assertions: Uses multiple parallel code-reviewers because the deduped review scope has `>5` files.
Partitions the files into non-overlapping groups by module, directory, or logical area so each file appears in exactly one reviewer scope.
Keeps the reviewer file lists manager-authored instead of telling reviewers to recompute or narrow scope.
Waits for every reviewer to return `DONE` before the final tracking sync.
